title: 2022 Pacs attack: One accused pleads guilty
description: GEORGE NEWS - The seven men accused of...
bodyText:
GEORGE NEWS - The seven men accused of attacking Maximilian Reimann (20) in Pacaltsdorp in the early hours of Saturday morning, 27 August 2022, appeared in the George Regional Court today, Thursday 29 August.


> Six of the accused were asked to leave the courtroom while the 20-year-old Johnwill Tarentaal, pleaded guilty to a charge of assault with the intent to cause grievous bodily harm (GBH).


In his plea, Tarentaal admitted to an altercation with Reimann in front of a nightclub in Pacaltsdorp on the night of the attack. He said that Reimann accused him of stealing his cigarettes and they got into a fight where he was knocked unconscious.

He said he did not know for how long he was unconscious, but when he woke up he saw that there was a group of people fighting further down the road. He walked over and kicked Reimann who was involved in the fight.


> Tarentaal will be back in court on 29 October pending pre-sentencing reports.


The other six, Chaywin van Niekerk (18), Shannen-Lee Botha (18), Jody Botha (21), Sheldon Remas (19), Ethan Bevie (21) and Chadwill Kiewiet (19) will make their next court appearance on 2 September in the Thembalethu Regional Court.

All seven accused were denied bail in September 2022 and have been in custody since their arrest shortly after the incident.

Reimann was attacked by a group of men in Mission Street, Pacaltsdorp, in the early hours of the morning. A passer-by, Charlotte Edwards, found him lying next to the road later that morning.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
